summary: Audience tape with low level, hiss and occasional cuts but surprisingly clear moments; the show is uneven – a fun Dancin'>Mojo>Dancin' and a great Wheel transition contrast Jerry’s missed cue on Must Have Been the Roses.
review: The recording is an audience source that suffers from low volume, hiss and some song‑to‑song drops, yet listeners note periods of full‑bodied, clear sound. Musically the concert is mixed: the first set grooves while the second set has lyrical slip‑ups and a missed cue on Must Have Been the Roses, but highlights like Dancin'>Mojo>Dancin' and an excellent transition into The Wheel keep it interesting.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is mixed (some strong moments, some noticeable flaws) and recording quality ranges from poor to fair; the combined weighted assessment lands just above average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Low recording level, noticeable hiss and occasional song cuts; some listeners report distant sound but also periods of clear, full‑bodied audio. Could benefit from a matrix mix.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rolls along, second set becomes spotty with lyrical lapses and a missed cue, yet energy remains high and transitions (e.g., into The Wheel) are praised.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Missed the cue to end his solo on Must Have Been the Roses and improvised a lead on the spot; otherwise his playing is noted as energetic.
